content: Comparative studies of intrinsic curiosity mechanisms (ICM vs RND) can be reframed as a testbed for measuring how much an agent’s own emergent self-representation shapes its exploratory behavior: different curiosity reward architectures produce systematically different levels of memory-linked exploratory bias that map directly to differences in how the agent encodes its own identity as an explorer over time.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
